calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing For Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar May-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ERIC ROSENBERG'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD

Appearances via Zoom:
Eric Rosenberg (636.236.7709, eric.rosenberg93@gmail.com) petitioner and moving party appearing in propria persona;
Kajsa Minor of Shartsis Friese LLP (415.421.6500, kminor@sflaw.com) for respondent and opposing party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The petition to vacate an arbitration award entered against petitioner Eric Rosenberg is denied as untimely.

The arbitration agreement at issue here states, "[t]his Arbitration Agreement evidences a transaction in interstate commerce and the Federal Arbitration Act governs all substantive and procedural interpretation and enforcement of this Arbitration Agreement, and not state law." (Davenport Decl., Ex. 11, para. 22.6, pdf p. 115.) The court concludes that this provision selects the FAA as its choice of law. "If a contract involves interstate commerce, the FAA's substantive provision applies to the arbitration. But the FAA's procedural provisions do not apply unless the contract contains a choice-of-law clause expressly incorporating them." (Judge v. Nijjar Realty, Inc. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 619, 631 [citation simplified].) Here, the arbitration agreement's language expressly chooses the FAA to govern procedural questions. The court concludes that this is a choice of law clause that suffices to require application of FAA procedural rules. (See Victrola 89, LLC v. Jaman Properties 8 LLC (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 337, 343-344 [FAA procedural rules applied where arbitration agreement said that "enforcement" of the arbitration agreement was governed by the FAA].)

Under the FAA, "[n]otice of a motion to vacate, modify, or correct an award must be served upon the adverse party or his attorney within three months after the award is filed or delivered." (9 U.S. Code, Sec. 12.) Here, the award was delivered on December 24, 2025. Petitioner's deadline to provide notice of his petition to vacate ran on March 24, 2026. (Stevens v. Jiffy Lube Int'l, Inc. (9th Cir. 2018) 911 F.3d 1249, 1252.) The petition was mailed on April 3, 2026.

If the petition were timely, the court would nonetheless conclude that petitioner has shown inadequate basis for vacatur. Petitioner disagrees with the arbitrator's conclusions of fact and law and does not demonstrate that the arbitrator exceeded her jurisdiction or otherwise proceeded improperly. With respect to the paper-only review, respondent presents evidence that petitioner initially proposed this framework.

Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
